legal.summary: Committee members must disclose conflicts of interest and recuse themselves from affected deliberations.

requirements.requirement: Any Committee member with a direct or indirect interest in a matter under discussion must disclose the fact and nature of the interest to the Committee
requirements.rationale: Ensures governance integrity of NCII designation and policy decisions at the highest level

requirements.requirement: Disclosed interests must be recorded in the minutes of the Committee meeting
requirements.rationale: Creates auditable record of governance compliance

technical.summary: No technical requirements for this governance provision.

governance.summary: Conflict-of-interest management framework for the national cyber security governance body.

requirements.requirement: Member must recuse from all deliberation and decision-making on the disclosed matter after making the disclosure
requirements.rationale: Prevents conflicted decision-making on NCII designations and sector determinations

legal.summary: CE directives are binding instruments that NCII entities and sector leads must comply with.

requirements.requirement: Monitor, identify and maintain a register of all CE directives issued under the Act, ensuring awareness of each directive's requirements and effective date
requirements.rationale: CE directives form a critical layer of the regulatory framework — non-compliance with any directive constitutes an offence under s18

requirements.requirement: Ensure organizational compliance with each directive within specified implementation timelines
requirements.rationale: Directives published in Gazette have binding legal force on all NCII entities

technical.summary: Technical compliance with the specific requirements of each CE directive.

requirements.requirement: Implement technical controls and configurations specified in each applicable CE directive
requirements.rationale: Directives may mandate specific technical measures such as incident reporting formats, risk assessment methodologies, or security configurations

governance.summary: Governance framework for tracking and implementing CE directives.

requirements.requirement: Establish a directive tracking and compliance monitoring process, including gap analysis for each new directive and remediation planning
requirements.rationale: Systematic approach needed given approximately 10 directives already published with more expected

s17.title: Designation of NCII entity and NCII sector lead

legal.summary: Gateway provision for all Part IV obligations. Designation as an NCII entity triggers the full compliance duty catalogue.

requirements.requirement: Identify whether the organization owns or operates infrastructure that could qualify as NCII and proactively assess designation risk
requirements.rationale: Once designated, the entity bears immediate obligations under ss18-26 with severe penalties for non-compliance

requirements.requirement: Upon receiving a designation notice, verify the scope of designated NCII assets and understand the full compliance obligations triggered
requirements.rationale: Designation scope determines which systems are subject to risk assessment, audit, and incident notification requirements

requirements.requirement: If aggrieved by designation, file an appeal to the Minister within 30 days of notification
requirements.rationale: Strict 30-day time limit for appeals; failure to appeal within this period results in designation becoming final

technical.summary: Asset identification and scoping for NCII designation.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a comprehensive asset inventory identifying all systems that may qualify as NCII, including their criticality assessment and interdependencies
requirements.rationale: CE considers the nature, extent, and consequences of compromise — organizations need to understand their own NCII landscape

requirements.requirement: Map system dependencies and identify single points of failure for designated NCII assets
requirements.rationale: Understanding the blast radius of infrastructure compromise is essential for both designation assessment and subsequent compliance

governance.summary: Board-level awareness and governance for NCII designation and its implications.

requirements.requirement: Brief the Board on NCII designation implications including full compliance obligations, penalty exposure (up to RM500,000/10 years), and director liability under s52
requirements.rationale: Board must understand that designation creates personal liability for directors and officers

requirements.requirement: Appoint a designated NCII compliance officer and establish clear accountability for Act compliance
requirements.rationale: Cross-functional compliance program requires dedicated ownership given breadth of obligations

legal.summary: Comprehensive duty catalogue for NCII entities. Breach of any duty is a criminal offence (RM500,000/10 years).

requirements.requirement: Implement and maintain cyber security measures to protect NCII, ensuring measures are current, effective, and proportionate to the risk profile
requirements.rationale: Overarching obligation encompassing all technical and organizational measures for NCII protection

requirements.requirement: Comply with all applicable standards, codes of practice, and directives issued under the Act
requirements.rationale: s18(1)(b) creates a legal obligation to comply with subsidiary instruments, making non-compliance with any standard/CoP/directive a criminal offence

requirements.requirement: Report cyber security incidents to the CE in accordance with s26 notification requirements
requirements.rationale: s18(1)(c) duty to report, read with s26 6-hour timeline for significant incidents

requirements.requirement: Conduct or cause to be conducted cyber security risk assessments per s21 requirements
requirements.rationale: s18(1)(d) duty read with s21 for prescribed frequency and approved assessor requirement

requirements.requirement: Conduct or cause to be conducted cyber security audits per s23 requirements
requirements.rationale: s18(1)(e) duty read with s23 for prescribed frequency and approved auditor requirement

requirements.requirement: Provide information as required by the CE or NCII sector lead under s20
requirements.rationale: s18(1)(f) duty to cooperate with regulatory information requests

technical.summary: Technical implementation of NCII protection measures across the full cyber security lifecycle.

requirements.requirement: Deploy and maintain defence-in-depth security architecture for all designated NCII assets, including network segmentation, access controls, encryption, and monitoring
requirements.rationale: Duty to implement and maintain cyber security measures requires a comprehensive technical security program

requirements.requirement: Implement 24/7 security monitoring capability for designated NCII, with alerting and escalation for potential incidents
requirements.rationale: 6-hour incident notification window requires real-time detection capability

requirements.requirement: Maintain current patch management and vulnerability remediation processes for NCII assets
requirements.rationale: Known vulnerabilities in NCII assets could constitute failure to maintain cyber security measures

governance.summary: Governance framework for NCII entity duties and compliance program management.

requirements.requirement: Establish a comprehensive NCII compliance program with clear policies, procedures, roles and responsibilities covering all s18(1) duties
requirements.rationale: Multiple concurrent obligations require an integrated compliance management approach

requirements.requirement: Report quarterly to the Board on NCII compliance status, covering risk assessment results, audit findings, incident history, and remediation progress
requirements.rationale: Director liability under s52 requires boards to demonstrate active oversight of NCII compliance

legal.summary: Sector leads must coordinate, monitor, and support NCII entities within their sector.

requirements.requirement: Coordinate and monitor the cyber security posture of all NCII in the designated sector
requirements.rationale: Sector leads are the regulatory intermediary between NACSA and individual NCII entities

requirements.requirement: Develop and recommend sector-specific codes of practice for CE approval under s22
requirements.rationale: CoPs tailor the Act's general requirements to sector-specific contexts and risk profiles

requirements.requirement: Report to the CE on the state of cyber security in the sector
requirements.rationale: Feeds into national-level situational awareness and policy development

technical.summary: Technical coordination and advisory functions of sector leads.

requirements.requirement: Establish sector-level threat intelligence sharing mechanism and coordinate sectoral incident response
requirements.rationale: Sector leads must be technically capable of monitoring and coordinating cyber security across their sector

governance.summary: Sector-level governance framework for NCII oversight.

requirements.requirement: Establish a sector-level NCII governance forum for regular engagement with NCII entities, including compliance support and best-practice sharing
requirements.rationale: s19(1)(c) duty to advise NCII entities and s19(1)(e) duty to assist compliance require structured engagement

legal.summary: NCII entities must provide information and documents when requested by the CE, with criminal penalties for non-compliance or providing false information.

requirements.requirement: Respond to CE information requests within the specified period, providing complete and accurate information about NCII assets, security posture, and operations
requirements.rationale: Non-compliance carries RM200,000/3 years; false information carries RM300,000/5 years

requirements.requirement: Establish internal processes to collect and verify NCII information rapidly, ensuring responses can meet CE-specified timelines
requirements.rationale: Information requests may have tight deadlines; organizations need ready access to asset inventories, configuration data, and security documentation

technical.summary: Technical capability to produce accurate NCII information on demand.

requirements.requirement: Maintain up-to-date NCII asset register with system configurations, network diagrams, data flow maps, and security control inventory
requirements.rationale: CE information requests may require detailed technical documentation about NCII assets

governance.summary: Governance processes for handling regulatory information requests.

requirements.requirement: Establish a regulatory request handling procedure with defined roles, escalation paths, and quality assurance checks to ensure accuracy of responses
requirements.rationale: Criminal liability for false or misleading information requires robust verification before submission

legal.summary: Mandatory periodic and on-demand risk assessments by approved persons, with results submitted to CE and sector lead.

requirements.requirement: Conduct cyber security risk assessments at intervals not exceeding the prescribed period (typically annually) for all designated NCII
requirements.rationale: Non-compliance carries RM500,000/10 years — one of the highest penalty tiers under the Act

requirements.requirement: Conduct additional risk assessments within the period specified when directed by the CE
requirements.rationale: CE may direct ad-hoc assessments in response to emerging threats, sector-wide incidents, or compliance concerns

requirements.requirement: Engage only CE-approved persons to conduct the risk assessment
requirements.rationale: Assessment quality assurance — approved persons are typically licensed cyber security service providers under Part V

requirements.requirement: Submit risk assessment results to the CE and the relevant NCII sector lead within prescribed timeframes
requirements.rationale: Results inform NACSA's national NCII risk picture and the sector lead's oversight function

technical.summary: Technical execution of cyber security risk assessment methodology.

requirements.requirement: Apply the risk assessment methodology specified in the Cyber Security (Risk Assessment and Audit) Regulations 2024, covering threat identification, vulnerability assessment, impact analysis, and risk evaluation for all NCII assets
requirements.rationale: Methodology must align with CE-prescribed standards to ensure consistency across the national NCII landscape

requirements.requirement: Include assessment of supply chain risks, third-party dependencies, and interconnected infrastructure in the NCII risk assessment scope
requirements.rationale: NCII risk assessments must consider the full attack surface, not just internal systems

requirements.requirement: Develop and implement a risk treatment plan addressing all identified risks, with clear timelines, resource allocation, and accountable owners for each treatment action
requirements.rationale: Risk assessment is only valuable if findings are acted upon; treatment plan demonstrates due diligence

governance.summary: Governance framework for risk assessment program management.

requirements.requirement: Present risk assessment results and treatment plan to the Board, obtaining formal approval and resource commitment for remediation activities
requirements.rationale: Director liability (s52) requires board-level engagement with NCII risk findings

requirements.requirement: Track risk treatment plan implementation progress and report on residual risk status to the CE/sector lead as required
requirements.rationale: Ongoing accountability for risk remediation beyond the assessment itself

legal.summary: NCII entities must comply with CE-approved codes of practice applicable to their sector.

requirements.requirement: Identify all codes of practice approved under s22 that apply to the organization's NCII sector and ensure full compliance
requirements.rationale: Non-compliance with CoPs is a criminal offence (RM200,000/3 years)

requirements.requirement: Monitor for CE variations, revocations, or replacements of applicable codes of practice and implement changes within specified timeframes
requirements.rationale: CoPs may be updated as threats evolve; entities must maintain compliance with current versions

technical.summary: Implementation of sector-specific CoP technical requirements.

requirements.requirement: Implement the technical standards, security controls, and best practices specified in the applicable sector CoP
requirements.rationale: CoPs translate the Act's general cyber security duty into sector-specific, actionable technical requirements

requirements.requirement: Conduct periodic gap analysis between current practices and CoP requirements, maintaining a compliance status register
requirements.rationale: Structured tracking ensures sustained compliance and identifies gaps proactively before audit

legal.summary: Mandatory periodic and on-demand cyber security audits by approved persons, with results submitted to CE and sector lead.

requirements.requirement: Conduct cyber security audits at intervals not exceeding the prescribed period (typically annually) for all designated NCII
requirements.rationale: Non-compliance carries RM500,000/10 years — same penalty tier as risk assessment failure

requirements.requirement: Conduct additional audits within the period specified when directed by the CE
requirements.rationale: CE may direct audits in response to incidents, risk assessment findings, or compliance concerns

requirements.requirement: Engage only CE-approved persons to conduct the audit
requirements.rationale: Audit quality assurance — approved auditors must hold valid licences under Part V

requirements.requirement: Submit audit results to the CE and the relevant NCII sector lead within prescribed timeframes
requirements.rationale: Audit results inform regulatory oversight and sectoral compliance monitoring

technical.summary: Technical scope and execution of cyber security audit.

requirements.requirement: Audit scope must cover compliance with the Act, regulations, directives, applicable codes of practice, and standards determined by the CE under s24
requirements.rationale: Comprehensive audit scope ensures the full regulatory framework is assessed, not just technical controls

requirements.requirement: Include technical testing (vulnerability assessment, penetration testing, configuration review) as part of the audit, proportionate to the NCII risk profile
requirements.rationale: Audit must go beyond document review to verify actual security posture of NCII assets

governance.summary: Governance for audit program management and remediation tracking.

requirements.requirement: Present audit findings to the Board and develop a remediation plan with clear timelines for addressing each finding
requirements.rationale: Board oversight of audit findings demonstrates due diligence; unaddressed findings create ongoing compliance risk

requirements.requirement: Track remediation progress for all audit findings and ensure closure within agreed timelines, with escalation for overdue items
requirements.rationale: Demonstrating remediation effort is critical for subsequent audit cycles and regulatory engagement

legal.summary: NCII entities must comply with CE-determined cyber security standards within specified timeframes.

requirements.requirement: Comply with all cyber security standards determined by the CE under s24(1) within the implementation period specified
requirements.rationale: Non-compliance is a criminal offence (RM200,000/3 years)

requirements.requirement: Conduct gap analysis between current security posture and each applicable CE-determined standard, implementing required controls within the specified compliance deadline
requirements.rationale: Standards may reference international frameworks (ISO 27001, NIST CSF) or prescribe specific technical configurations

requirements.requirement: Maintain a standards compliance register tracking the organization's status against each applicable CE-determined standard
requirements.rationale: Multiple standards may apply; structured tracking ensures nothing falls through the cracks

s25.title: Duty to provide information on threats

legal.summary: NCII entities must share threat information that could affect other NCII with the CE as soon as practicable.

requirements.requirement: Report to the CE as soon as practicable any information relating to a cyber security threat or incident that may affect other NCII beyond the entity's own infrastructure
requirements.rationale: Enables cross-sector early warning and coordinated response; non-compliance carries RM200,000/3 years

technical.summary: Technical capability for threat intelligence sharing.

requirements.requirement: Establish processes and channels to identify, assess and rapidly share threat intelligence relevant to other NCII with the CE, including indicators of compromise, attack patterns, and vulnerability disclosures
requirements.rationale: Sharing must be timely ('as soon as practicable') — requires automation and pre-established communication channels

governance.summary: Governance for threat intelligence sharing decisions.

requirements.requirement: Define criteria and escalation procedures for determining when threat information may affect other NCII and triggers the s25 sharing obligation
requirements.rationale: Employees need clear guidance on what constitutes a reportable threat to avoid both under-reporting and over-reporting

legal.summary: NCII entities must notify the CE of cyber security incidents within 6 hours (significant impact) or other prescribed periods. Heaviest penalties in Part IV.

requirements.requirement: Notify the CE within 6 hours of becoming aware of a cyber security incident that has a significant impact on NCII
requirements.rationale: Most time-critical obligation under the Act; non-compliance carries RM500,000/10 years plus RM5,000/day continuing offence

requirements.requirement: For incidents without significant impact, notify the CE within the period prescribed by regulations
requirements.rationale: Non-significant incidents still require notification, though with a longer timeline

requirements.requirement: Include all prescribed information in the incident notification, ensuring accuracy and completeness
requirements.rationale: False or misleading notification is a separate offence (RM300,000/5 years under s26(4))

technical.summary: Technical incident detection and notification capability.

requirements.requirement: Deploy SIEM, IDS/IPS, EDR, and other detection technologies capable of identifying cyber security incidents affecting NCII in near-real-time
requirements.rationale: 6-hour clock starts at 'becoming aware' — organizations must have capability to detect incidents rapidly

requirements.requirement: Establish pre-configured incident notification templates and secure communication channels to NACSA, enabling sub-6-hour notification
requirements.rationale: Notification templates reduce response time and ensure prescribed information fields are not missed during high-pressure incident response

requirements.requirement: Implement incident classification framework with clear criteria for determining 'significant impact' triggering the 6-hour notification window
requirements.rationale: Rapid triage is essential — the clock starts at awareness, not at classification

requirements.requirement: Maintain a documented, tested incident response plan (IRP) with clear roles, escalation procedures, and notification workflows aligned with the 6-hour timeline
requirements.rationale: IRP must be operationally ready — not just documented — with regular tabletop exercises and drills

requirements.requirement: Ensure 24/7 availability of personnel authorized to approve and send incident notifications to the CE
requirements.rationale: 6-hour notification window applies regardless of time of day; requires on-call arrangements and delegated authority

requirements.requirement: Conduct post-incident reviews for all notified incidents, documenting lessons learned and implementing improvements
requirements.rationale: Continuous improvement of incident response capability reduces future risk and demonstrates mature governance

s28.title: Licensing of cyber security service providers

legal.summary: Mandatory licensing for all persons providing prescribed cyber security services.

requirements.requirement: Obtain a valid licence from the CE before providing any cyber security service as defined in s2
requirements.rationale: Unlicensed provision is a criminal offence (RM500,000/10 years)

requirements.requirement: Comply with all conditions imposed on the licence by the CE
requirements.rationale: Licence conditions may include qualifications, staffing, insurance, and operational requirements

technical.summary: Technical competency requirements for licensed service providers.

requirements.requirement: Maintain the technical competencies, certifications, tools, and infrastructure required by licence conditions for each licensed service category
requirements.rationale: Licensed services include SOC monitoring, penetration testing, and incident response — each requiring specific technical capabilities

governance.summary: Governance for licensing compliance by service providers.

requirements.requirement: For NCII entities: verify that all engaged cyber security service providers hold valid licences under Part V before engagement and throughout the service period
requirements.rationale: Engaging unlicensed providers may constitute failure to comply with s18 duties

legal.summary: Licensed cyber security service providers must maintain professional standards, confidentiality, and records.

requirements.requirement: Maintain standards of competence, integrity and professionalism in the provision of all cyber security services
requirements.rationale: Professional standards obligation applies to all licensed activities

requirements.requirement: Maintain strict confidentiality of all information obtained in the course of providing cyber security services
requirements.rationale: Service providers have deep access to client NCII; unauthorized disclosure could compromise national security

requirements.requirement: Keep and maintain comprehensive records of all cyber security services provided, including scope, findings, and recommendations
requirements.rationale: Records support regulatory oversight and audit trail for services rendered to NCII entities

technical.summary: Technical obligations for licensed service providers.

requirements.requirement: Implement information security controls to protect client information, including encryption, access controls, and secure data handling procedures
requirements.rationale: Confidentiality obligation requires technical protection of sensitive NCII data obtained during service delivery

requirements.requirement: Implement a quality management system covering staff competency, service delivery standards, record keeping, and compliance with CE directives and guidelines
requirements.rationale: Systematic approach to meeting the broad professional obligations under s32

legal.summary: Entities must not obstruct authorized officers exercising enforcement powers.

requirements.requirement: Cooperate fully with authorized officers conducting investigations, inspections, or enforcement activities under Part VI, providing requested access, documents, and information
requirements.rationale: Obstruction carries RM300,000/5 years; includes refusing access, hindering officers, refusing to produce documents, or refusing to answer questions

requirements.requirement: Be prepared to provide passwords, encryption keys, decryption codes, and any other means required for authorized officers to access computerized data during searches (per s37)
requirements.rationale: Failure to provide technical access constitutes obstruction under s44(c)

requirements.requirement: Establish a documented procedure for interacting with NACSA authorized officers during inspections and investigations, including designated points of contact and escalation to legal counsel
requirements.rationale: Structured response prevents accidental obstruction while protecting the organization's rights

legal.summary: Information acquired under the Act must be kept confidential; unauthorized disclosure is a criminal offence.

requirements.requirement: Ensure all persons performing functions under the Act maintain strict confidentiality of information acquired in the course of those functions
requirements.rationale: Breach of secrecy carries RM500,000/10 years — one of the highest penalties under the Act

requirements.requirement: Only disclose Act-related information for permitted purposes: purposes of the Act, civil/criminal proceedings, or with written CE consent
requirements.rationale: Three narrow exceptions define the boundaries of permitted disclosure

technical.summary: Technical controls for protecting Act-related information.

requirements.requirement: Implement information classification and handling procedures for Act-related data (incident reports, risk assessments, audit results, threat intelligence), with access restricted to authorized personnel
requirements.rationale: Technical controls must enforce the secrecy obligation across all systems and channels

requirements.requirement: Include Act secrecy obligations in staff training, employment contracts, and NDA arrangements for all personnel with access to Act-related information
requirements.rationale: Staff awareness and contractual commitments are the first line of defence against unauthorized disclosure

legal.summary: Directors and officers face personal criminal liability for corporate offences unless they prove due diligence. Corporate fines may be multiplied by 10x.

requirements.requirement: Directors, CEO, COO, managers and officers must be able to demonstrate that they exercised all due diligence to prevent the commission of any offence by the body corporate
requirements.rationale: Reverse burden of proof — officers are deemed to have committed the offence unless they can prove due diligence and lack of knowledge/consent/connivance

requirements.requirement: Maintain evidence of board-level engagement with NCII compliance, including meeting minutes, compliance reports reviewed, decisions made, and resources allocated
requirements.rationale: Due diligence defence requires demonstrable evidence of active governance, not passive awareness

technical.summary: No direct technical requirements; s52 is a liability provision.

governance.summary: Board governance framework to support due diligence defence.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Board Cyber Security Committee or assign NCII compliance oversight to an existing board committee with regular reporting cadence
requirements.rationale: Structured board oversight is the foundation of a credible due diligence defence under s52

requirements.requirement: Ensure adequate resources (budget, staffing, tools) are allocated for NCII compliance, with board-approved budget for cyber security risk treatment
requirements.rationale: Demonstrating resource allocation is key evidence of due diligence by directors
